steps.title: Understand what weakens a prediction claim
steps.content: The student claims opening weekend earnings can reliably predict Oscar recognition. To weaken such a claim, you need data showing a case where opening weekend earnings fail to produce the predicted outcome.

steps.title: Find the counterexample
steps.content: Choice A shows the needed counterexample: The Girl Who Played with Fire had much higher opening weekend earnings (~7.5 million) than Crouching Tiger, Hidden Dragon (~5.9 million), yet wasn't nominated for an Oscar while Crouching Tiger was, proving that opening weekend earnings don't reliably predict Oscar recognition. Choices B, C, and D either compare unrelated metrics (lifetime earnings, not opening weekend), contain inaccurate data, or address irrelevant factors. That matches choice A — A is correct.
